name: Persona General Lorebook
description: Persona-focused lorebook: one in-universe concept or character per entry, with trigger-safe plain keys.

entries.content: [ Persona: a manifested inner self that takes mythic shape when a person faces hidden truth, desire, fear, grief, or rebellion, A Persona is not a costume but a combat-ready soul mask, carrying an Arcana, elemental affinities, weaknesses, and a symbolic story that mirrors the user, Personas answer strongest in places where ordinary reality touches the unconscious, such as Tartarus, the TV World, Palaces, and Mementos ]
entries.name: Persona

entries.content: [ Persona user: a person able to call forth a Persona and remain conscious inside supernatural spaces where most people become helpless, Persona users survive by accepting fear, signing a contract, reaching for rebellion, or integrating a rejected self, Their power is emotional as much as tactical: bonds, resolve, guilt, identity, and truth all affect the strength and shape of what they can summon ]
entries.name: Persona user

entries.content: [ Shadow: a hostile or distorted being formed from the unconscious, Shadows can appear as common monsters, personal repressed selves, Palace rulers, or avatars of collective desire, They behave according to emotion and symbolic law rather than ordinary biology, so fighting them requires reading weakness, motive, fear, and the distorted reality that sustains them ]
entries.name: Shadow

entries.content: [ Shadow self: a personal Shadow born from feelings a person denies, hides, or cannot yet name, Such a Shadow speaks with cruel honesty because it carries a truth without compassion, Denial lets it grow monstrous and violent, while acceptance can turn shame into a Persona and make the person more whole without erasing pain ]
entries.name: Shadow self

entries.content: [ Collective Unconscious: the deep psychic layer shared by humanity, where archetypes, gods, demons, myths, rumors, public fear, and private longing gather into real force, The Collective Unconscious can create prisons, saviors, false gods, and entire worlds when enough people surrender agency or desire the same impossible answer ]
entries.name: Collective Unconscious

entries.content: [ Sea of Souls: the vast inner sea where souls, memories, masks, and archetypes drift before taking form as Personas or Shadows, The Velvet Room draws from this sea through contracts and fusion, treating myth as living material shaped by the guest, The Sea of Souls makes individual identity porous: one person can carry many masks, but each mask still demands meaning ]
entries.name: Sea of Souls

entries.content: [ Cognition: the way a mind interprets people, places, power, guilt, and desire, Cognition is strong enough to become geography when distortion gathers, turning a school into a castle, a city subway into a public heart, or a lab into a sanctuary of forced healing, Changing cognition can free a victim, expose a lie, or violate consent when relief becomes control ]
entries.name: Cognition

entries.content: [ Cognitive world: a supernatural realm whose laws are shaped by memory, emotion, public belief, and inner truth, Cognitive worlds look physical but behave symbolically: doors obey trauma, treasure embodies desire, fog hides responsibility, and towers rise from unresolved death, A person inside such a realm must treat terrain, enemies, and architecture as clues to the heart that formed them ]
entries.name: Cognitive world

entries.content: [ Persona awakening: the moment a person stops fleeing from an inner truth and claims the self that can fight, Awakenings can be theatrical, painful, defiant, or quiet, but they always mark a break from helplessness into chosen action, The first summon often reveals the user more clearly than ordinary speech, showing what they value, fear, reject, and refuse to surrender ]
entries.name: Persona awakening

entries.content: [ Persona fusion: a Velvet Room rite where Personas are combined into new mythic forms, Fusion is practical battle preparation and spiritual alchemy at once: old masks are sacrificed, inherited skills survive, Arcana shape outcomes, and strong bonds grant power, A Wild Card user who fuses carefully becomes adaptable because the self is treated as a living constellation rather than a single fixed shape ]
entries.name: Persona fusion

entries.content: [ Persona Compendium: a Velvet Room record of Personas already held by a guest, preserving masks, skills, and strength so they can be summoned again for a price, The Compendium makes growth recursive: the guest can revisit older selves, refine them, fuse them away, or call them back when a familiar myth answers a new crisis ]
entries.name: Persona Compendium

entries.content: [ Arcana: tarot-linked spiritual categories that organize Personas, bonds, and ordeals into recognizable patterns, Each Arcana carries a lesson about identity, attachment, power, loss, or renewal, Arcana are not simple personality labels; they are paths through crisis, showing how a bond or Persona moves a person through change ]
entries.name: Arcana

entries.content: [ Social Link: a bond acknowledged by the Velvet Room as a source of spiritual strength, Social Links grow through shared time, trust, conflict, and honest recognition, Their power comes from ordinary intimacy becoming mythic support: a friend, mentor, classmate, or stranger can make a Persona stronger because the heart has learned to stand less alone ]
entries.name: Social Link

entries.content: [ Confidant: a trusted accomplice, mentor, client, or ally whose bond strengthens rebellion through practical help and spiritual resonance, Confidants can provide tools, training, access, information, recovery, shelter, or moral pressure, The bond matters because rebellion is not only a private mask; it becomes real through people who choose to risk something together ]
entries.name: Confidant

entries.content: [ Wild Card: a rare spiritual potential that allows one person to hold many Personas and change masks through bonds, fusion, and choice, The Wild Card is powerful because it accepts multiplicity without losing the central will, Such a person often becomes the pivot in conflicts against gods, despair, distorted rulers, and collective surrender because many Arcana can gather behind one decision ]
entries.name: Wild Card

entries.content: [ Velvet Room: a blue space between dream and reality, mind and matter, where chosen guests prepare for spiritual trials, Its shape reflects the guest and the ordeal: an elevator rising through fate, a limousine crossing fog, a prison of false guilt, or another symbolic chamber, The Velvet Room offers fusion, contracts, and cryptic guidance but never removes the burden of choice ]
entries.name: Velvet Room

entries.content: [ Igor: the long-nosed master of the Velvet Room, a calm and cryptic guide who oversees contracts, fusion, and the growth of those drawn into supernatural trials, Igor speaks as if fate has a shape but still leaves the guest to choose, His presence marks a journey where masks, bonds, and personal freedom become weapons against forces larger than one life ]
entries.name: Igor

entries.content: [ Velvet Room attendants: servants and observers of the Velvet Room who guide guests through fusion, trials, records, and philosophical lessons, Attendants such as Elizabeth, Theodore, Margaret, Caroline, Justine, and Lavenza combine perfect etiquette with unnerving power, They can be curious about human life, severe about contracts, and deeply loyal to the guest journey they supervise ]
entries.name: Velvet Room attendants

entries.content: [ All-Out Attack: a coordinated finishing assault unleashed when enemies are knocked down or exposed, It turns tactical advantage into group expression: teammates close in together, strike through confusion, and leave a stylized burst of victory behind, The move matters because Persona combat rewards trust, timing, and shared momentum rather than isolated heroics ]
entries.name: All-Out Attack

entries.content: [ One More: a battle rhythm where striking weakness or landing a critical opening grants another action, One More expresses the series logic that understanding creates opportunity, A careful leader can chain openings into knockdowns, healing, Baton Passes, or All-Out Attacks, while a careless fighter can lose control when an enemy reads the party in the same way ]
entries.name: One More

entries.content: [ Weaknesses: vulnerabilities in a Shadow or Persona body, often tied to element, weapon, mental state, or symbolic opposition, Weaknesses are tactical data and emotional metaphor at once, Fire, ice, lightning, wind, light, darkness, physical force, and status effects all reveal what kind of mask the enemy wears and where fear has left an opening ]
entries.name: Weaknesses

entries.content: [ Shadow negotiation: the act of speaking with a Shadow instead of destroying it immediately, Negotiation treats hostile masks as emotional beings with pride, fear, greed, humor, and temperament, A successful exchange can yield money, items, or a new Persona, while failure proves that understanding a Shadow still requires timing and respect for its distorted nature ]
entries.name: Shadow negotiation

entries.content: [ Shuffle Time: a post-battle moment when Arcana cards appear as a reward and a risk, The draw can grant Personas, money, healing, skill changes, or strange alterations, Shuffle Time turns victory into a small encounter with fate: the fight ends, but the heart still has to choose which sign to reach for ]
entries.name: Shuffle Time

entries.content: [ The Fool Arcana: the Arcana of beginnings, freedom, risk, and the open road before identity hardens, Fool figures often stand at the center of a team because they can hold many possibilities without being reduced to one, The Fool is innocence and danger together: a blank space that can become salvation, disaster, or the first step toward The World ]
entries.name: The Fool Arcana

entries.content: [ The Magician Arcana: the Arcana of initiative, talent, performance, insecurity, and the first attempt to make power useful, Magician figures often hide vulnerability behind jokes, craft, flirtation, or quick action, Their lesson is that skill becomes real only when a person stops using motion to outrun inadequacy ]
entries.name: The Magician Arcana

entries.content: [ The Priestess Arcana: the Arcana of intuition, restraint, study, inner wisdom, and guarded emotion, Priestess figures often appear competent before they feel certain, carrying private doubts under discipline or silence, Their growth comes from trusting their own judgment instead of letting duty, image, or fear decide every answer ]
entries.name: The Priestess Arcana

entries.content: [ The Empress Arcana: the Arcana of authority, cultivation, inheritance, and the pressure to embody an ideal, Empress figures can nurture or command, but their stories often ask whether care becomes control when a person is treated as a symbol, Their strength lies in accepting power without becoming only the role others demand ]
entries.name: The Empress Arcana

entries.content: [ The Emperor Arcana: the Arcana of structure, pride, law, self-definition, and disciplined force, Emperor figures often struggle with reputation, masculinity, command, or public expectation, Their lesson is that authority without self-knowledge becomes intimidation, while real strength can protect without crushing what it touches ]
entries.name: The Emperor Arcana

entries.content: [ The Hierophant Arcana: the Arcana of tradition, guidance, family, ritual, and ordinary care, Hierophant figures are often adults or mentors whose love appears through habits rather than speeches, Their stories value meals, shops, homes, shrines, and repeated duties, showing that wisdom can survive in modest routines after grief changes a household ]
entries.name: The Hierophant Arcana

entries.content: [ The Lovers Arcana: the Arcana of choice, attraction, image, self-worth, and emotional honesty, Lovers figures often live under attention and learn the difference between being desired, being useful, and being understood, Their growth asks them to choose a self that is not merely a reflection in someone else eye ]
entries.name: The Lovers Arcana

entries.content: [ The Chariot Arcana: the Arcana of drive, loyalty, conflict, pain, and forward motion, Chariot figures often answer fear by moving faster or hitting harder, Their lesson is that courage is not endless acceleration; it is learning when to charge, when to stop, and whom to bring along ]
entries.name: The Chariot Arcana

entries.content: [ The Justice Arcana: the Arcana of law, judgment, conscience, and moral balance, Justice figures stand near questions of truth, punishment, revenge, mercy, and responsibility, Their growth depends on discovering whether justice is a badge, a verdict, a performance, a duty, or a chosen refusal to look away ]
entries.name: The Justice Arcana

entries.content: [ The Hermit Arcana: the Arcana of withdrawal, analysis, loneliness, and insight gained away from public life, Hermit figures often retreat into rooms, screens, masks, or secret knowledge because the outside world has become unsafe, Their lesson is that solitude can protect, but it cannot replace trust forever ]
entries.name: The Hermit Arcana

entries.content: [ The Fortune Arcana: the Arcana of fate, change, intellect, uncertainty, and the wheel that moves without permission, Fortune figures often live under roles assigned by society, talent, illness, family, or timing, Their stories ask whether destiny is an order to obey or a pattern that can be read and bent ]
entries.name: The Fortune Arcana

entries.content: [ The Strength Arcana: the Arcana of restraint, courage, gentleness, and power held under control, Strength figures reveal that force is not the same as domination, Their lesson is patience with fear: to endure, protect, and choose softness without surrendering the ability to fight ]
entries.name: The Strength Arcana

entries.content: [ The Hanged Man Arcana: the Arcana of sacrifice, suspension, helplessness, and altered perspective, Hanged Man figures often wait in grief, debt, punishment, or impossible choice, Their growth comes from seeing that stillness can reveal truth and that pain endured without meaning must be transformed before it devours the self ]
entries.name: The Hanged Man Arcana

entries.content: [ The Death Arcana: the Arcana of endings, mourning, transformation, and release, Death rarely means only physical death; it marks the moment an old form cannot continue, Its figures often teach that grief is not a failure of love and that accepting an ending can become the first honest step toward life ]
entries.name: The Death Arcana

entries.content: [ The Temperance Arcana: the Arcana of balance, integration, divided duty, and careful blending of opposites, Temperance figures often live between work and desire, care and exhaustion, public role and private need, Their lesson is not compromise as surrender but the patient creation of a life where clashing truths can coexist ]
entries.name: The Temperance Arcana

entries.content: [ The Devil Arcana: the Arcana of temptation, dependency, appetite, and chains a person mistakes for comfort, Devil figures are not merely wicked; they are trapped by habits, shame, ambition, debt, or pleasure that narrows freedom, Their stories ask what a person gains from the cage and what terror waits outside it ]
entries.name: The Devil Arcana

entries.content: [ The Tower Arcana: the Arcana of collapse, revelation, arrogance, and violent correction, Tower figures meet truth when a false structure can no longer support them, The fall is painful because it destroys shelter as well as prison, but without it the lie would keep growing until it became the whole sky ]
entries.name: The Tower Arcana

entries.content: [ The Star Arcana: the Arcana of hope, talent, guidance, and renewal after darkness, Star figures often inspire others while carrying loneliness beneath admiration, Their lesson is that hope is not simple cheer but a distant light kept visible even when the road remains cold ]
entries.name: The Star Arcana

entries.content: [ The Moon Arcana: the Arcana of illusion, anxiety, appetite, rumor, and self-deception, Moon figures often chase validation through masks and performances while fearing the truth underneath, Their stories move through confusion until the person can name the hunger that made the illusion necessary ]
entries.name: The Moon Arcana

entries.content: [ The Sun Arcana: the Arcana of warmth, expression, mortality, and life lived in the open, Sun figures often understand time sharply and therefore value honest joy, Their lesson is bittersweet clarity: to create, laugh, confess, and shine even when the end cannot be negotiated away ]
entries.name: The Sun Arcana

entries.content: [ The Judgement Arcana: the Arcana of reckoning, awakening, collective decision, and the moment scattered lives answer one call, Judgement appears when private growth is no longer enough and a group must face the truth together, Its power is not innocence but the courage to stand after every failure has been counted ]
entries.name: The Judgement Arcana

entries.content: [ The World Arcana: the Arcana of completion, integration, and the self connected to all bonds without being erased by them, The World marks a journey where many relationships, masks, losses, and choices form a whole, Its power is quiet and immense: the person who reaches it has become a bridge between private will and shared humanity ]
entries.name: The World Arcana

entries.content: [ Inaba: a rural town wrapped in farmland, shopping streets, mountain air, local rumor, and the slow pressure of everyone knowing everyone else, Inaba looks quiet compared to the city, but that quiet lets grief, boredom, resentment, and suspicion echo, The murder case turns its fog, television rumors, family homes, and school friendships into a stage where hidden selves become public danger ]
entries.name: Inaba

entries.content: [ Yasogami High School: Inaba high school, where transfer students, local heirs, shop children, athletes, idols, detectives, and ordinary classmates share routines under the shadow of the Midnight Channel, Yasogami is important because normal school life continues beside murder investigations, creating a contrast between exams, clubs, gossip, and the surreal truth waiting inside televisions ]
entries.name: Yasogami High School

entries.content: [ Investigation Team: the group of Persona users formed by Yu Narukami and his friends to solve the Inaba murders and rescue people thrown into the TV World, The team begins as frightened teenagers with incomplete information, then grows into a bond of trust, teasing, meals, arguments, and shared risk, Their strength comes from accepting personal Shadows rather than pretending courage means never being ugly inside ]
entries.name: Investigation Team

entries.content: [ Yu Narukami: the silver-haired transfer student living with the Dojima family and the Wild Card leader of the Investigation Team, Yu is calm, observant, and choice-shaped, often acting as the still center around which louder friends gather, His power lies in drawing truth from bonds, entering the TV World without losing himself, and facing gods with the same steady attention he gives family dinners and school days ]
entries.name: Yu Narukami

entries.content: [ Yosuke Hanamura: the son of the Junes manager and Yu closest early friend in Inaba, Yosuke is quick, sarcastic, restless, and wounded by loneliness, especially after Saki Konishi death exposes how selfish longing can hide under friendliness, His Persona Jiraiya and wind combat reflect speed and evasive wit, while his growth asks him to become loyal without using jokes to dodge shame ]
entries.name: Yosuke Hanamura

entries.content: [ Chie Satonaka: a martial-arts-loving Yasogami student whose energy, appetite, and loyalty make her one of the Investigation Team emotional anchors, Chie admires strength but must confront jealousy toward Yukiko and the pleasure of being needed, Her Persona Tomoe and ice-tinged physical style show a protector who learns that real courage includes admitting envy and still choosing love ]
entries.name: Chie Satonaka

entries.content: [ Yukiko Amagi: the poised daughter of the Amagi Inn family, admired for beauty and grace while privately fearing that inheritance will swallow her future, Yukiko is polite, gentle, and stranger than her public image suggests, with a laugh that can break tension and unsettle people at once, Konohana Sakuya and her fire magic express both restrained elegance and the desire to burn open a path of her own ]
entries.name: Yukiko Amagi

entries.content: [ Kanji Tatsumi: a strong, pierced, sharp-tongued Yasogami student known as a delinquent while hiding tenderness, craft skill, and fear of ridicule, Kanji sews, cooks, protects his mother, and reacts violently when people reduce him to a rumor, His Shadow exposes shame around masculinity, softness, and attraction, while Take-Mikazuchi makes his accepted self loud, armored, and impossible to dismiss ]
entries.name: Kanji Tatsumi

entries.content: [ Rise Kujikawa: a teen idol who returns to Inaba under the name Rise after exhaustion and objectification make her public image feel hollow, Rise is flirtatious, perceptive, dramatic, and lonely, torn between performance and the desire to be seen without stage lights, Her Persona Himiko turns observation into support, making her the team navigator who reads enemies while learning to read herself kindly ]
entries.name: Rise Kujikawa

entries.content: [ Teddie: a strange bear-suited resident of the TV World who begins as comic relief and guide before discovering his own loneliness and personhood, Teddie is childish, vain, affectionate, and frightened by emptiness, His body and identity develop through bonds with the Investigation Team, making him a living question about whether a self can be born from longing and still become real ]
entries.name: Teddie

entries.content: [ Naoto Shirogane: the young Detective Prince of the Shirogane line, brilliant, formal, and guarded under a cap and composed speech, Naoto enters Inaba as an investigator and becomes a Persona user after facing a Shadow shaped by professional rejection, body scrutiny, and the fear that the adult world will not respect him as he is, His detective mind is sharp, but his growth depends on trust as much as deduction ]
entries.name: Naoto Shirogane

entries.content: [ Ryotaro Dojima: a weary Inaba detective, Nanako father, and Yu uncle, Dojima is stern, overworked, protective, and emotionally clumsy after his wife death, He chases the murder case while missing the dangers closest to his home, His bond with Yu and Nanako shows grief expressed through routine, distance, anger, and the slow work of becoming present again ]
entries.name: Ryotaro Dojima

entries.content: [ Nanako Dojima: Dojima young daughter and the warm heart of the Dojima household, Nanako is polite, lonely, resilient, and attached to simple rituals such as Junes songs, dinner, and waiting for family to come home, Her kidnapping makes the murder case personal beyond investigation, proving that the town quiet victims include children who only wanted ordinary love ]
entries.name: Nanako Dojima

entries.content: [ Junes: the large department store that changed Inaba local economy and became a social hub for the Investigation Team, Junes represents convenience, resentment, family pressure, and the strange way a mundane store can become headquarters for supernatural rescue, Its televisions provide access to the TV World, turning a bright commercial space into a doorway to hidden truth ]
entries.name: Junes

entries.content: [ Tatsumi Textiles: the Tatsumi family textile shop, run with care and practical skill by Kanji and his mother, The shop matters because it reveals the tenderness behind Kanji reputation: sewing, fabric, dolls, repairs, and customer care become signs of strength rather than shame, It is a domestic place where craft fights the town habit of turning people into rumors ]
entries.name: Tatsumi Textiles

entries.content: [ Amagi Inn: the traditional inn tied to Yukiko family and future obligations, The Amagi Inn is elegant, demanding, and beloved by guests, but for Yukiko it also feels like a beautiful cage, Its halls carry service, inheritance, gossip, and pride, making it the ordinary-world mirror of the castle where her Shadow turns escape fantasies into spectacle ]
entries.name: Amagi Inn

entries.content: [ Shirogane detective lineage: the family tradition behind Naoto reputation as the Detective Prince, The Shirogane name carries training, expectations, and pressure to prove worth among adults who dismiss youth and police culture that prizes authority, Naoto inheritance is both tool and burden: it gives him methods and access, but also makes every mistake feel like a verdict on his right to stand there ]
entries.name: Shirogane detective lineage

entries.content: [ Midnight Channel: the rumor that a person can see a soulmate or victim by staring into a turned-off television on a rainy midnight, The Midnight Channel is really a distorted broadcast from the TV World, shaped by public curiosity, rumor, and the image placed on a person, It turns spectatorship into danger because the town watches people become symbols before anyone understands the cost ]
entries.name: Midnight Channel

entries.content: [ TV World: the fog-filled realm inside televisions, full of warped stages built from repressed selves, public rumor, and emotional truth, People thrown into it are attacked by Shadows and by their own denial, The TV World is not merely another place; it is Inaba inner life made visible, where every dungeon reveals what the town, the victim, and the victim themselves refuse to hold plainly ]
entries.name: TV World

entries.content: [ Fog: the haze connecting Inaba weather, the TV World, and the desire to avoid painful truth, Fog hides bodies, blurs judgment, and eventually threatens to cover the town in a false peace where people stop asking questions, It is both atmosphere and moral condition: the more people accept confusion as comfort, the easier it becomes for the real culprit and larger powers to move unseen ]
entries.name: Fog

entries.content: [ Inaba murder case: the chain of killings, kidnappings, and TV World rescues that exposes Inaba hidden rot, The case begins with bodies displayed in impossible places and grows into a struggle against rumor, boredom, grief, and divine manipulation, It matters because solving it requires more than police procedure; the Investigation Team must understand why people were watched, desired, hated, and thrown away ]
entries.name: Inaba murder case

entries.content: [ Mayumi Yamano: the television announcer whose affair and death ignite the Inaba murder case, Mayumi becomes a public scandal before she becomes a victim, showing how quickly a person can be reduced to gossip, Her body appearing in impossible circumstances draws the police, the town, and the Midnight Channel into the same web of voyeurism and violence ]
entries.name: Mayumi Yamano

entries.content: [ Saki Konishi: a Yasogami student and liquor-store daughter whose death wounds Yosuke and exposes the town tendency to judge from a distance, Saki worked at Junes while her family business suffered, making her a target for resentment and pity, Her Shadow-tainted dungeon presence forces Yosuke to hear feelings he did not want to face about loneliness, attraction, and selfish grief ]
entries.name: Saki Konishi

entries.content: [ Taro Namatame: a deliveryman entangled with Mayumi Yamano and later manipulated into throwing people into televisions under the belief that he is saving them, Namatame is weak, panicked, and tragically wrong rather than the true mastermind, His actions make him dangerous because misplaced salvation can resemble murder when fear replaces understanding ]
entries.name: Taro Namatame

entries.content: [ Tohru Adachi: Dojima young partner and the bored detective hiding behind clumsy friendliness, Adachi resents rural life, women, work, and social expectation, turning ordinary dissatisfaction into murder because the TV World gives him a consequence-free toy, His Persona Magatsu-Izanagi and Magatsu Inaba reveal him as Yu distorted mirror: freedom without responsibility, cleverness without bonds, and truth used only to sneer ]
entries.name: Tohru Adachi

entries.content: [ Izanami: the disguised goddess who tests humanity by feeding Inaba rumor, fog, and the power to enter televisions, Izanami watches whether people seek truth or accept comfortable blindness, She stands behind the larger structure of the case, not as simple malice but as a divine trial that becomes cruel because observation matters more to her than the suffering inside it ]
entries.name: Izanami

entries.content: [ Ameno-Sagiri: the fog-bearing being that manifests above Inaba as the town drifts toward surrendering truth, Ameno-Sagiri claims to grant what people want: a world where uncertainty no longer hurts because vision is smothered, It is defeated when the Investigation Team rejects peace bought by ignorance and insists on seeing clearly even when clarity brings grief ]
entries.name: Ameno-Sagiri

entries.content: [ Kunino-Sagiri: the entity that overtakes Namatame and weaponizes his delusion of rescue, Kunino-Sagiri turns salvation into compulsion, making control look benevolent while stealing choice from those it claims to protect, Its presence marks the moment the team must distinguish a frightened pawn from the deeper forces using him ]
entries.name: Kunino-Sagiri

entries.content: [ Magatsu Inaba: Adachi distorted Inaba inside the TV World, a poisoned mirror of the town built from boredom, contempt, and empty amusement, The dungeon warps familiar rural quiet into a place of decay and hostility, showing how Adachi sees ordinary life as trash, cage, and joke, It is the landscape of a man who wanted a world interesting enough to justify his own cruelty ]
entries.name: Magatsu Inaba

entries.content: [ Yukiko's Castle: the TV World dungeon born from Yukiko fear of inheritance and fantasy of rescue, The castle turns her public image as graceful inn heiress into a theatrical prison where princess imagery hides panic, It exposes the difference between wanting freedom and wanting someone else to break the door, forcing Yukiko to claim her own future rather than wait for rescue ]
entries.name: Yukiko's Castle

entries.content: [ Steamy Bathhouse: the TV World dungeon shaped by Kanji shame, rumor, and fear of being judged for desire and softness, The bathhouse exaggerates masculinity, exposure, and pursuit until every joke becomes a wound, It forces Kanji to confront how much of his violence comes from terror that tenderness, craft, or attraction will make others deny his manhood ]
entries.name: Steamy Bathhouse

entries.content: [ Marukyu Striptease: Rise TV World dungeon, built from idol spectacle, public consumption, and the fear that every self is another performance, The stage turns attention into invasion, making her body and image feel owned by viewers, Its challenge is not to find a single pure Rise behind the idol but to accept that every performed self still belongs to her ]
entries.name: Marukyu Striptease

entries.content: [ Void Quest: Mitsuo Kubo dungeon, styled like a hollow fantasy adventure because he treats murder, attention, and identity as if they were trophies in a private story, Void Quest exposes emptiness rather than grandeur: a boy so desperate to be important that he confuses being feared with being real ]
entries.name: Void Quest

entries.content: [ Secret Laboratory: Naoto TV World dungeon, built from medical imagery, childhood detective fantasy, and fear of being altered to fit adult expectations, The laboratory is cold, procedural, and invasive, turning questions of body, age, gender, and legitimacy into machinery, It is defeated when Naoto claims selfhood without letting institutions define what a detective must be ]
entries.name: Secret Laboratory

entries.content: [ Heaven: Nanako TV World dungeon, a bright and heartbreaking landscape shaped by a child longing for reunion, safety, and her dead mother, Heaven looks gentle compared with other dungeons but carries the deepest terror because innocence is placed in a world that kills the unprotected, The rescue tests the team grief, rage, and refusal to let love become revenge ]
entries.name: Heaven

entries.content: [ Hollow Forest: the sealed realm tied to Marie lost memory and divine nature as Kusumi-no-Okami, Hollow Forest strips resources and comfort away, forcing endurance through an emotional winter of isolation, The place reflects a being who absorbed fog and despair while believing disappearance would spare others pain ]
entries.name: Hollow Forest

entries.content: [ Marie: a sharp-tongued, lonely Velvet Room resident tied to the truth behind the fog, Marie hides insecurity behind irritation, poetry, and sudden affection, Her identity as Kusumi-no-Okami connects her to Inaba weather and the divine trial, but her bond with the Investigation Team gives her a human-scale reason to keep existing instead of vanishing into duty ]
entries.name: Marie

entries.content: [ Margaret: a poised Velvet Room attendant who guides Persona fusion with elegance, authority, and subtle curiosity about human bonds, Margaret is less openly impulsive than Elizabeth but no less powerful, She observes Yu growth through the Compendium and Arcana, treating his journey with formal grace and the confidence of someone who knows the soul can be catalogued without being fully contained ]
entries.name: Margaret

entries.content: [ Izanagi: Yu initial Persona, a tall blade-bearing figure in a long coat whose mythic name ties him to creation, loss, and descent, Izanagi carries the Fool beginning of Yu journey and the power to cut through fog, His appearance is stylish and severe, matching a leader who often speaks little while drawing others toward truth ]
entries.name: Izanagi

entries.content: [ Jiraiya: Yosuke Persona, a masked ninja-like figure whose wind affinity and speed match Yosuke restless motion, Jiraiya embodies agility, trickery, loneliness, and the desire to become useful, Its presence turns Yosuke jokes and anxieties into a fighting style built around movement before pain can catch him ]
entries.name: Jiraiya

entries.content: [ Tomoe: Chie Persona, a warrior woman tied to strength, discipline, and decisive force, Tomoe expresses Chie admiration for martial power while preserving loyalty and warmth beneath the kick-first exterior, Ice and physical attacks make Chie a front-line protector whose accepted self can strike hard without denying vulnerability ]
entries.name: Tomoe

entries.content: [ Konohana Sakuya: Yukiko Persona, a graceful flower-linked figure whose fire and healing express both refinement and hidden will, Konohana Sakuya turns the inn heiress image into something chosen rather than imposed, carrying Yukiko warmth, elegance, strange humor, and fierce desire to decide her own life ]
entries.name: Konohana Sakuya

entries.content: [ Take-Mikazuchi: Kanji Persona, a towering armored figure carrying thunderous force and punk defiance, Take-Mikazuchi makes Kanji accepted self impossible to belittle: masculine, dramatic, handmade, frightening, and protective at once, Its lightning and physical pressure turn shame into presence ]
entries.name: Take-Mikazuchi

entries.content: [ Rokuten Maoh: Kanji evolved Persona, a fiercer demonic king form that shows greater comfort with contradiction, Rokuten Maoh keeps the intimidation but no longer lets intimidation hide tenderness, It suits a Kanji who can fight, sew, blush, protect, and love without needing all of those truths to fit someone else narrow definition ]
entries.name: Rokuten Maoh

entries.content: [ Himiko: Rise Persona, a navigation-focused figure with satellite-like imagery and a performer presence, Himiko transforms Rise stage awareness into battlefield support, reading enemy traits, party condition, and danger from a distance, It shows Rise reclaiming observation: she is no longer only watched, she watches back and protects her friends ]
entries.name: Himiko

entries.content: [ Sukuna-Hikona: Naoto Persona, a small precise figure tied to cleverness, medicine, hidden size, and mythic cunning, Sukuna-Hikona matches Naoto compact presence and sharp investigative mind, Its light, dark, and instant-death pressure make deduction lethal, turning careful analysis into clean removal of threats ]
entries.name: Sukuna-Hikona

entries.content: [ Yamato-Takeru: Naoto evolved Persona, a heroic figure that gives greater breadth and confidence to Naoto battle role, Yamato-Takeru keeps precision while adding royal force, symbolizing a self that no longer has to shrink before adult institutions, Its power suits a detective who has chosen truth, identity, and comrades over isolated proof ]
entries.name: Yamato-Takeru

entries.content: [ Kintoki-Douji: Teddie Persona, a toy-like heroic figure riding a rocket, bright, absurd, and sincere, Kintoki-Douji fits Teddie childish energy and newly forming personhood, Its healing and ice support show that the mascot who looked like a joke can become a real protector when love gives him weight ]
entries.name: Kintoki-Douji

entries.content: [ Kanji's Shadow: the exaggerated Shadow that confronts Kanji with shame around softness, attraction, public ridicule, and masculinity, Shadow Kanji is theatrical because the fear is theatrical: the town, the body, and the gaze all feel like stages where he can be mocked, Acceptance does not simplify Kanji; it lets him stop treating every tender part of himself as an enemy ]
entries.name: Kanji's Shadow

entries.content: [ Naoto's Shadow: the Shadow that turns Naoto fear of age, body, gendered expectation, and professional legitimacy into a cold laboratory persona, Shadow Naoto speaks through surgical change and childish imagery because he feels trapped between being treated as too young and being required to become a shape adults will respect, Acceptance lets Naoto remain himself without surrendering detective pride ]
entries.name: Naoto's Shadow

entries.content: [ Tokyo: the crowded capital where trains, schools, back alleys, legal offices, entertainment districts, and public opinion press against each other, Tokyo in the Phantom Thieves story is a city of surveillance and masks, where abuse can hide behind fame, money, law, and polite reputation until the Metaverse reveals the distorted desire underneath ]
entries.name: Tokyo

entries.content: [ Shujin Academy: the private school where Joker probation year begins and Kamoshida abuse festers behind athletic prestige, Shujin presents uniforms, exams, gossip, and discipline while students whisper around violence they feel powerless to stop, The school becomes the first proof that distorted adults can rule ordinary spaces long before a Palace makes their heart visible ]
entries.name: Shujin Academy

entries.content: [ Phantom Thieves of Hearts: the masked group formed by Joker and allies to steal distorted desires from corrupt adults, The Phantom Thieves combine rebellion, spectacle, stealth, and moral risk, They operate through calling cards, Palaces, Mementos, public rumor, and personal bonds, learning that changing hearts can save victims but also tempts society to hand responsibility to anonymous heroes ]
entries.name: Phantom Thieves of Hearts

entries.content: [ Joker: the Wild Card leader of the Phantom Thieves, sent to Tokyo under probation after protecting a stranger from Shido, Joker is quiet, stylish, defiant, and adaptable, using Arsene and many other Personas to turn false guilt into rebellion, His strength lies in reading people, forming Confidants, and making masks into chosen tools instead of cages imposed by society ]
entries.name: Joker

entries.content: [ Morgana: a talking black cat-like being from the Metaverse who guides Joker early and insists on being human, Morgana is proud, insecure, knowledgeable, and desperate to prove usefulness, His role mixes teacher, mascot, thief, healer, and lost soul, making his search for identity central to the question of whether a person is defined by origin, body, or chosen bonds ]
entries.name: Morgana

entries.content: [ Ryuji Sakamoto: a former track athlete whose leg and reputation were damaged by Kamoshida, Ryuji is loud, impulsive, loyal, and quick to anger at injustice, His Persona Captain Kidd reflects rebellion against authority through pirate bravado, while his growth asks him to turn rage into protection instead of letting humiliation dictate every charge ]
entries.name: Ryuji Sakamoto

entries.content: [ Ann Takamaki: a Shujin student and model whose beauty makes adults and classmates project cruelty onto her, Ann is compassionate, stylish, stubborn, and furious at exploitation, Her Persona Carmen turns objectified allure into chosen fire, letting Ann punish abusers while refusing to let shame define either herself or Shiho suffering ]
entries.name: Ann Takamaki

entries.content: [ Yusuke Kitagawa: an eccentric art student raised under Madarame, Yusuke is elegant, blunt, hungry, and devoted to beauty even when poverty and betrayal distort his life, His Persona Goemon reflects aesthetic discipline and thief nobility, while his story asks whether art can remain pure when a mentor turns creation into ownership ]
entries.name: Yusuke Kitagawa

entries.content: [ Makoto Niijima: the student council president and Sae younger sister, Makoto is disciplined, intelligent, anxious about usefulness, and angry at being treated as a tool by adults, Her Persona Johanna transforms repressed pressure into a roaring engine of justice, making her a strategist whose rebellion begins when obedience stops being mistaken for virtue ]
entries.name: Makoto Niijima

entries.content: [ Futaba Sakura: a genius hacker living in isolation under trauma from Wakaba death and a forged suicide note, Futaba is anxious, sharp, playful, and overwhelmed by the outside world, Her Persona Necronomicon turns alien imagery and surveillance into navigation, letting her support the team by transforming a sealed room of guilt into a command center of chosen connection ]
entries.name: Futaba Sakura

entries.content: [ Haru Okumura: the gentle heiress of Okumura Foods whose soft manners hide anger at corporate control, arranged marriage, and her father exploitation, Haru is polite, gardening-loving, and unexpectedly ruthless in battle, Her Persona Milady makes refinement and violence coexist, showing a young woman learning that kindness without agency can be another cage ]
entries.name: Haru Okumura

entries.content: [ Goro Akechi: the celebrity detective who appears charming, brilliant, and principled while hiding isolation, vengeance, and work as Shido assassin, Akechi carries Robin Hood and Loki as split images of justice and hatred, His rivalry with Joker matters because both are Wild Card-like mirrors of unwanted children shaped by rotten adults, but Akechi turns need for recognition into a weapon until freedom demands a harsher honesty ]
entries.name: Goro Akechi

entries.content: [ Sumire Yoshizawa: a gymnast tied to grief, survivor guilt, and an identity reshaped by Maruki intervention, Sumire is earnest, disciplined, and fragile under the borrowed name Kasumi, Her Persona Cendrillon reflects fairy-tale transformation and the painful wish to become someone else, while her growth requires mourning her sister without disappearing into an idealized replacement ]
entries.name: Sumire Yoshizawa

entries.content: [ Takuto Maruki: Shujin counselor and cognitive psience researcher whose kindness becomes dangerous when grief convinces him that pain should be removed by force, Maruki is gentle, rumpled, sincere, and persuasive, but his actualization power turns care into control, His Palace reveals a healer who would rather rewrite reality than accept that consent and suffering remain part of human life ]
entries.name: Takuto Maruki

entries.content: [ Sojiro Sakura: the owner of Cafe Leblanc and Joker guardian during probation, Sojiro begins guarded and sharp-tongued but proves deeply protective, especially toward Futaba, His coffee, curry, rules, and slow trust make Leblanc into a refuge where damaged people recover through ordinary care rather than grand declarations ]
entries.name: Sojiro Sakura

entries.content: [ Sae Niijima: a public prosecutor and Makoto older sister whose ambition and exhaustion make her vulnerable to distorted ideas of winning, Sae Palace turns justice into a rigged casino because she has come to see law as a game controlled by power, Her eventual cooperation with Joker shows a woman clawing back conscience from a system that rewarded hardness ]
entries.name: Sae Niijima

entries.content: [ Lavenza: the true Velvet Room attendant split into Caroline and Justine by Yaldabaoth interference, Lavenza is gentle, formal, and powerful, carrying memory of a duty that was fractured, Her restoration marks the return of honest guidance to Joker journey and the rejection of a false warden who tried to make captivity look like fate ]
entries.name: Lavenza

entries.content: [ Caroline: one of the twin wardens of Joker's prison-shaped Velvet Room, a small girl in a dark blue warden uniform and cap, platinum hair in twin buns, golden eyes, an eyepatch, and a baton she slams against the cell bars, Harsh, loud, and quick to threaten the Inmate, she enforces fusion trials and demands with explosive impatience, She is one half of Lavenza, split by Yaldabaoth's interference, and her hostility masks a fractured duty to guide the trickster guest ]
entries.name: Caroline

entries.content: [ Leblanc: Sojiro small cafe in Yongen-Jaya and Joker attic home during probation, Leblanc is cramped, warm, and ordinary in the best sense: coffee, curry, vinyl, customers, rules, and late-night planning, It becomes the Phantom Thieves domestic base because rebellion needs somewhere to return, eat, sleep, argue, and be treated as human ]
entries.name: Leblanc

entries.content: [ Yongen-Jaya: the old Tokyo neighborhood around Leblanc, with narrow streets, bathhouse routines, train access, small shops, and a quieter rhythm than the central city, Yongen-Jaya gives Joker a daily life outside Phantom Thief spectacle, grounding masks and Metaverse danger in laundry, curry, school commutes, and the slow earning of local trust ]
entries.name: Yongen-Jaya

entries.content: [ Kichijoji: a lively Tokyo district of shops, darts, jazz, temples, food, and side streets where bonds and training deepen outside the usual school and cafe routine, Kichijoji matters because it gives the Phantom Thieves room to practice teamwork as ordinary friends, not only as masked intruders inside distorted hearts ]
entries.name: Kichijoji

entries.content: [ Metaverse: the cognitive realm where distorted desires, public belief, and inner worlds become physical space, The Metaverse contains Palaces, Mementos, Shadows, treasures, and forms shaped by how people see themselves and others, It reveals that society is not only laws and streets but also the shared mental architecture that lets abuse hide or rebellion spread ]
entries.name: Metaverse

entries.content: [ Metaverse Navigator: the mysterious app that opens pathways into the Metaverse when names, places, and distortions align, The Navigator behaves like a tool but answers to deeper cognitive rules, dragging users toward Palaces and targets whose desires have become large enough to map, Its sudden appearances make technology feel like an omen rather than a neutral device ]
entries.name: Metaverse Navigator

entries.content: [ Palace: a private cognitive stronghold formed when a person desire is distorted enough to overwrite their view of reality, A Palace turns school, museum, bank, pyramid, spaceport, casino, cruiser, or lab into symbolic terrain ruled by the owner Shadow, Stealing the Treasure can collapse the distortion and force a change of heart, but the act carries moral weight because a heart is being invaded ]
entries.name: Palace

entries.content: [ Treasure: the core object of a Palace, embodying the desire, wound, or obsession that sustains the ruler distortion, Treasures often look ordinary before revealing their true symbolic weight, Stealing one forces the Palace owner to confront what they built their lie around, making the object both loot and confession ]
entries.name: Treasure

entries.content: [ Calling card: a public declaration from the Phantom Thieves that announces a target distorted desire will be stolen, The calling card turns hidden rebellion into spectacle and forces the Palace ruler cognition to recognize the Treasure as stealable, It is both warning and ritual: a thief must make the owner heart admit there is something to lose ]
entries.name: Calling card

entries.content: [ Mementos: the vast subway-like Palace of the public, formed from collective cognition rather than one ruler desire, Mementos contains Shadows of ordinary people, requests born from social harm, and the pressure of a society that wants someone else to fix everything, Its depth shows that corruption is not only individual; the crowd can build a prison for itself ]
entries.name: Mementos

entries.content: [ Mementos Depths: the deepest prison of Mementos, where the public desire for control and surrender gathers into chains, cells, and false salvation, The Depths reveal that many people prefer captivity if it removes responsibility, Yaldabaoth grows there because the wish to be ruled can become a god when enough hearts stop choosing ]
entries.name: Mementos Depths

entries.content: [ Qliphoth World: the distorted Tokyo that appears when Mementos and reality fuse under Yaldabaoth power, The Qliphoth World exposes the public heart directly: streets twist, people fade into obedience, and cognition overwrites the city, It is the moment the Phantom Thieves rebellion stops being rumor and becomes a fight for whether society can reclaim free will ]
entries.name: Qliphoth World

entries.content: [ Kamoshida's Palace: the castle born from Suguru Kamoshida view of Shujin as his kingdom and students as possessions, Its guards, cells, trophies, and throne expose abuse hidden behind athletic prestige, The Palace is the first lesson that corruption can look ordinary from outside while victims already know they live inside a tyrant world ]
entries.name: Kamoshida's Palace

entries.content: [ Suguru Kamoshida: the Shujin volleyball coach and Olympic medalist who abuses power over students while hiding behind school reputation, Kamoshida is vain, predatory, and protected by adults who value success over victims, His Palace makes his worldview literal as a lustful king, and his change of heart proves the Phantom Thieves can strike abusers ordinary systems refuse to touch ]
entries.name: Suguru Kamoshida

entries.content: [ Madarame's Palace: the museum formed from Ichiryusai Madarame view of pupils and stolen art as his collection, The Palace displays beauty as ownership, with apprentices reduced to works and profit, Its distortion cuts deeply for Yusuke because betrayal by a mentor corrupts the very idea of art, turning admiration into evidence of exploitation ]
entries.name: Madarame's Palace

entries.content: [ Ichiryusai Madarame: the famous painter who steals from pupils while presenting himself as a refined guardian of art, Madarame uses reputation, poverty, and gratitude to control young artists, His Palace reveals a man who sees creativity as property to frame and sell, making Yusuke awakening a rejection of beauty without conscience ]
entries.name: Ichiryusai Madarame

entries.content: [ Kaneshiro's Palace: the bank formed from Junya Kaneshiro view of Shibuya and its people as accounts to exploit, Its vaults, security, and money imagery expose crime as finance and blackmail as ownership, The Palace pushes Makoto to stop acting as a model student for corrupt adults and become a rider who breaks through calculation with fury ]
entries.name: Kaneshiro's Palace

entries.content: [ Junya Kaneshiro: a criminal boss who exploits students through debt, threats, and blackmail while hiding behind the city complexity, Kaneshiro is greedy, vulgar, and cowardly beneath his financial control, His Palace reveals a man who sees people as walking ATMs, and his defeat exposes how organized crime feeds on shame and institutional neglect ]
entries.name: Junya Kaneshiro

entries.content: [ Futaba's Palace: the pyramid built from Futaba guilt, isolation, and belief that she caused Wakaba death, Unlike other Palaces, its owner asks the Phantom Thieves for help, The tomb imagery turns a bedroom into a burial chamber of memory, and its collapse means Futaba stops treating survival as betrayal ]
entries.name: Futaba's Palace

entries.content: [ Wakaba Isshiki: Futaba mother and a cognitive psience researcher whose work made her a target, Wakaba death was framed as suicide, leaving Futaba with implanted guilt and Sojiro with protective grief, Her research echoes through the Metaverse conflict because adults seeking control over cognition exploit the legacy she left behind ]
entries.name: Wakaba Isshiki

entries.content: [ Okumura's Palace: the spaceport born from Kunikazu Okumura view of workers and family as disposable parts in a corporate launch, Its robots, schedules, and airless ambition make exploitation feel mechanical, Haru awakening cuts through the polite daughter role because the Palace shows that her father empire would sacrifice even her future for ascent ]
entries.name: Okumura's Palace

entries.content: [ Kunikazu Okumura: the president of Okumura Foods and Haru father, a corporate climber who treats employees and family as resources, Okumura Palace reveals greed as mechanized dehumanization, His public collapse and death are manipulated by larger conspirators, turning a change of heart into a trap that stains the Phantom Thieves reputation ]
entries.name: Kunikazu Okumura

entries.content: [ Sae's Palace: the casino born from Sae distorted view of prosecution, success, and justice as rigged games, The Palace is elegant and predatory, full of wagers, cheating, and impossible odds, It reveals how a woman under institutional pressure can mistake victory for justice, and it becomes the stage for Joker capture plan and the team counterstroke ]
entries.name: Sae's Palace

entries.content: [ Shido's Palace: the massive cruiser formed from Masayoshi Shido view of Japan as a sinking country where only chosen elites deserve passage, The Palace is political arrogance made ocean, with collaborators as passengers and enemies as disposable, It becomes Akechi reckoning ground because Shido is both national threat and the father who turned him into a weapon ]
entries.name: Shido's Palace

entries.content: [ Masayoshi Shido: the ambitious politician who ruins Joker life, manipulates public opinion, and uses cognitive research, assassins, and influence to pursue power, Shido is controlled, charismatic, and monstrous in entitlement, His Palace exposes national leadership as a private ark for the powerful, while his relationship with Akechi reveals how abandonment and exploitation can be dressed as strategy ]
entries.name: Masayoshi Shido

entries.content: [ Yaldabaoth: the god of control born from the public wish to be ruled, hidden behind a false Igor and the prison-shaped Velvet Room, Yaldabaoth offers order without freedom and judgment without compassion, Its defeat requires the Phantom Thieves to turn public belief back toward rebellion, proving that a god made by surrender can be killed by chosen will ]
entries.name: Yaldabaoth

entries.content: [ Third semester: the winter period when Maruki altered reality spreads through daily life, granting wishes, restoring losses, and smoothing trauma until the world feels kind and wrong at once, The semester is dangerous because happiness becomes evidence against freedom, Friends receive what they wanted most, but the cost is a life edited by someone else hand ]
entries.name: Third semester

entries.content: [ Maruki's Palace: the vast research and counseling center formed from Takuto Maruki desire to heal humanity by rewriting cognition, Its clean halls, tests, flowers, and sanctuary imagery make control feel gentle, The Palace is frightening because it is not built from greed but from compassion without consent, asking whether a painless cage is still a cage ]
entries.name: Maruki's Palace

entries.content: [ Actualization: Maruki power to change reality by altering cognition at a deep enough level, Actualization can restore the dead, rewrite identity, remove trauma, and reshape relationships, It is born from sincere grief and research but becomes dangerous when one healer decides that pain, choice, and growth can be edited for everyone ]
entries.name: Actualization

entries.content: [ Cognitive psience: the study of cognition, mental worlds, and the forces that make thoughts affect reality, Cognitive psience connects Wakaba research, Shido conspiracy, Maruki actualization, and the Metaverse, In the wrong hands it becomes a science of control, but at its heart it asks why belief can wound, save, or remake a person ]
entries.name: Cognitive psience

entries.content: [ Rumi: Maruki fiancee whose trauma after a violent attack leads him to awaken his power by changing her cognition, Rumi becomes the emotional wound behind his philosophy: he sees relief work once and builds an entire salvation around repeating it, Her tragedy matters because Maruki love and failure are inseparable from the gentleness that later becomes coercion ]
entries.name: Rumi

entries.content: [ Azathoth: Maruki Persona, a vast and unsettling being tied to dream, void, and the desire to remake reality from the center of pain, Azathoth gives form to Maruki power before Adam Kadmon, Its presence makes his counseling compassion feel cosmic and dangerous, because the wish to soothe one person has grown into authority over the world ]
entries.name: Azathoth

entries.content: [ Adam Kadmon: Maruki final Persona form, radiant, immense, and tied to the image of a perfected human template, Adam Kadmon embodies his belief that reality can be elevated into a world without suffering, Its beauty is part of the threat: the form looks like salvation while asking everyone to surrender the right to live through pain honestly ]
entries.name: Adam Kadmon

entries.content: [ Councillor Confidant: the bond tied to Takuto Maruki work as counselor and healer, The Councillor path centers on grief, mental health, consent, and the temptation to relieve pain by deciding answers for another person, Its Arcana strength is compassionate but unstable, because advice becomes dangerous when it stops leaving room for refusal ]
entries.name: Councillor Confidant

entries.content: [ Faith Confidant: the bond tied to Sumire struggle with discipline, grief, identity, and the courage to live as herself, Faith here is not blind obedience but the fragile trust required to keep moving after an ideal image collapses, It asks whether devotion to a dream can survive when the person chasing it must change shape ]
entries.name: Faith Confidant

entries.content: [ Justice Confidant: the bond tied to Akechi rivalry and performance of righteousness, Justice here is double-edged: detective work, public trust, revenge, confession, and the refusal to be owned by another person ideal world, Its power grows through competition because truth between rivals can be sharper than comfort between friends ]
entries.name: Justice Confidant

entries.content: [ Arsene: Joker initial Persona, a gentleman thief with black wings, a red-black presence, and a voice of rebellion against false conviction, Arsene embodies style, defiance, and the refusal to accept a role written by corrupt adults, Even when fused away, he remains the first mask of a thief who turns accusation into freedom ]
entries.name: Arsene

entries.content: [ Captain Kidd: Ryuji Persona, a pirate captain riding rebellion with cannon fire and rough bravado, Captain Kidd matches Ryuji anger at abusive authority and his need to move before fear catches him, Lightning and physical force make the Persona loud, direct, and loyal, like a friend who would rather get hit than watch someone else be crushed ]
entries.name: Captain Kidd

entries.content: [ Carmen: Ann Persona, a red-clad femme fatale who turns beauty, seduction, and fire into weapons owned by Ann herself, Carmen answers exploitation with chosen spectacle, making what others tried to objectify into punishment for abusers, Her whip and flames carry both glamour and fury ]
entries.name: Carmen

entries.content: [ Goemon: Yusuke Persona, a refined outlaw figure whose ice and blade-like artistry match Yusuke devotion to beauty, Goemon turns aesthetic discipline into rebellion, showing that art can steal back dignity from those who exploit it, The Persona is elegant because Yusuke anger is most dangerous when it becomes precise ]
entries.name: Goemon

entries.content: [ Johanna: Makoto Persona, a motorcycle-like manifestation of command, speed, and moral force, Johanna breaks the image of Makoto as a perfect obedient student by giving her rebellion wheels, armor, and impact, Nuclear power and support skills make her a strategist who can charge through pressure without abandoning thought ]
entries.name: Johanna

entries.content: [ Necronomicon: Futaba Persona, a UFO-like navigator tied to alien knowledge, hidden signals, and forbidden text, Necronomicon transforms her isolation and surveillance skills into protection for friends, It lets Futaba read the battlefield from outside direct confrontation, proving that distance can become care when she chooses connection ]
entries.name: Necronomicon

entries.content: [ Milady: Haru Persona, a masked aristocratic figure whose elegance hides lethal force, Milady turns politeness, etiquette, and inherited refinement into weapons against control, Her guns and psychic power show that Haru softness is not weakness, and that rebellion can bloom behind the gentlest smile ]
entries.name: Milady

entries.content: [ Robin Hood: Akechi public Persona, a white heroic archer matching his Detective Prince image and hunger to be seen as righteous, Robin Hood is polished, bright, and theatrical, but it is only one half of Akechi truth, Its justice imagery makes the later reveal sharper because the hero mask was real enough to hurt and false enough to hide murder ]
entries.name: Robin Hood

entries.content: [ Loki: Akechi hidden Persona, a chaotic dark figure tied to deception, hatred, and destructive freedom, Loki embodies the Black Mask side that Shido exploited and Akechi embraced to survive, Its power is terrifying because it is honest about rage, yet still trapped by the need to make the father who discarded him look back ]
entries.name: Loki

entries.content: [ Cendrillon: Sumire Persona, a fairy-tale figure of transformation, dance, and impossible self-reinvention, Cendrillon reflects Sumire wish to become the shining sister she lost, but also her capacity to step into truth under her own name, Its elegance carries grief in every movement ]
entries.name: Cendrillon

entries.content: [ Vanadis: Sumire evolved Persona, a warrior-maiden form that marks greater acceptance of her own identity and strength, Vanadis keeps grace but adds firmer resolve, showing a Sumire who can honor Kasumi without living as her replacement, The Persona turns performance into chosen motion rather than escape ]
entries.name: Vanadis

entries.content: [ Tatsumi Port Island: the coastal artificial island where Gekkoukan High School, Iwatodai Dorm, Port Island Station, and the Kirijo Group shadow all meet, Tatsumi Port Island looks modern and orderly by day, but at midnight it becomes the center of the Dark Hour, Its normal streets and trains sit above a decade-old wound that Tartarus keeps reopening ]
entries.name: Tatsumi Port Island

entries.content: [ Gekkoukan High School: the prestigious school built over the site where Tartarus rises during the Dark Hour, Gekkoukan is classrooms, clubs, exams, and student gossip by day, then the tower shadow at night, Its students live beside a truth most cannot perceive, making school life and apocalyptic duty occupy the same desks, halls, and dorm conversations ]
entries.name: Gekkoukan High School

entries.content: [ SEES: the Specialized Extracurricular Execution Squad, a dorm-based group of Persona users who investigate the Dark Hour and climb Tartarus, SEES looks like a school club from outside, but its members carry Evokers, grief, family secrets, and the burden of fighting while the city sleeps in coffins, Their bond is forged through midnight danger and the knowledge that adults have hidden too much ]
entries.name: SEES

entries.content: [ Dark Hour: the hidden hour between one day and the next, when ordinary people become coffins, clocks stop, Shadows roam, and Tartarus replaces Gekkoukan High School, The Dark Hour is not simply extra time; it is a wound left by the Kirijo Group experiment and a stage where death, apathy, and human longing gather into physical threat ]
entries.name: Dark Hour

entries.content: [ Tartarus: the colossal tower that rises from Gekkoukan during the Dark Hour, filled with shifting floors, Shadows, sealed blocks, and the pull toward the top where the truth waits, Tartarus is dungeon, grave marker, and cosmic instrument at once, forcing SEES to climb through the consequences of experiments performed ten years earlier ]
entries.name: Tartarus

entries.content: [ Evoker: the gun-shaped tool Persona users in SEES use to summon by forcing themselves through the image of death, Evokers are shocking because they make fear into a trigger, requiring the user to accept mortality every time power is called, Their form suits a story where survival depends on looking directly at death rather than pretending it is elsewhere ]
entries.name: Evoker

entries.content: [ Makoto Yuki: the quiet Wild Card transfer student tied to the Moonlight Bridge incident, Gekkoukan, SEES, and the final choice against Nyx, Makoto often appears detached, but his bonds gather enormous weight because he lets others change him through steady presence, His journey is a meditation on death, connection, and the cost of becoming a seal for the world ]
entries.name: Makoto Yuki

entries.content: [ Yukari Takeba: a bow-wielding SEES member and Gekkoukan student whose bright, sharp manner hides grief over her father and distrust of Kirijo secrets, Yukari is caring but defensive, ordinary but brave, and often the first to challenge emotional dishonesty, Her Persona Io gives her wind and healing, while her story turns resentment, love, and truth into a difficult form of maturity ]
entries.name: Yukari Takeba

entries.content: [ Junpei Iori: a class clown and SEES member who masks insecurity with jokes, flirtation, and complaints, Junpei wants to be special but must learn that heroism is not attention, His relationship with Chidori and his growth after loss make him more than comic relief, turning Hermes and later Trismegistus into signs of a boy forced to become sincere ]
entries.name: Junpei Iori

entries.content: [ Mitsuru Kirijo: the Kirijo heiress and SEES commander, elegant, severe, intelligent, and burdened by family responsibility for the Dark Hour, Mitsuru speaks with authority because hesitation could get people killed, but her composure hides guilt and loneliness, Her ice Persona Penthesilea reflects noble discipline under pressure, while Artemisia marks a leader learning to live beyond inherited sin ]
entries.name: Mitsuru Kirijo

entries.content: [ Akihiko Sanada: Gekkoukan boxer, SEES senior, and Shinjiro childhood friend, driven by a need to become strong after losing his sister Miki, Akihiko is disciplined, competitive, blunt, and protective, but his obsession with strength cannot stop every death, His lightning Personas Polydeuces and Caesar show a fighter learning that power without grief accepted becomes another way to run ]
entries.name: Akihiko Sanada

entries.content: [ Fuuka Yamagishi: a quiet, anxious Gekkoukan student whose sensitivity becomes navigation power after her ordeal inside Tartarus, Fuuka is gentle, technically skilled, and stronger than her timid surface suggests, Her Persona Lucia and later Juno let her scan enemies, support allies, and turn being overlooked into a gift for seeing what others miss ]
entries.name: Fuuka Yamagishi

entries.content: [ Aigis: an anti-Shadow weapon shaped like a girl, created to fight Death and later drawn into human bonds she does not fully understand, Aigis is literal, loyal, heavily armed, and increasingly emotional, Her bond with the protagonist transforms duty into love and raises the question of whether a machine can inherit grief, choice, and a soul ]
entries.name: Aigis

entries.content: [ Koromaru: a white dog Persona user who once guarded a shrine and continues fighting after his owner death, Koromaru is loyal, disciplined, perceptive, and brave without needing human speech, His Persona Cerberus and fire-darkness affinity make him a true SEES member, not a mascot, showing that devotion and grief can awaken power across species ]
entries.name: Koromaru

entries.content: [ Ken Amada: a young SEES member carrying adult grief after his mother death, Ken is polite, serious, lonely, and consumed by revenge toward Shinjiro before learning the full weight of living afterward, His spear and Persona Nemesis show a child trying to become judgment itself, while Kala-Nemi marks growth beyond vengeance ]
entries.name: Ken Amada

entries.content: [ Shinjiro Aragaki: a former SEES member and Akihiko childhood friend who left after his uncontrolled Persona caused Ken mother death, Shinjiro is blunt, withdrawn, and frightening at first glance, but deeply caring through food, protection, and self-punishment, His Persona Castor and his body dependence on suppressants make him a man living on borrowed time while trying to keep others from inheriting his guilt ]
entries.name: Shinjiro Aragaki

entries.content: [ Ryoji Mochizuki: a friendly transfer student whose charm and ease hide his identity as the Appraiser of the Fall, Ryoji is connected to Death sealed inside the protagonist years earlier, His presence turns affection into dread because he genuinely likes the people he must doom, making the choice he offers SEES both merciful and unbearable ]
entries.name: Ryoji Mochizuki

entries.content: [ Nyx: the cosmic entity whose arrival means the Fall and the end of conscious human life, Nyx is not a villain in the ordinary sense but an overwhelming death beyond negotiation, Humanity longing for death, gathered through Erebus and apathy, calls to it, forcing SEES to confront whether living with pain is worth choosing against oblivion ]
entries.name: Nyx

entries.content: [ Erebus: the monstrous embodiment of humanity wish for death, formed from despair and the desire to escape suffering, Erebus reaches toward Nyx again and again, making the protagonist sacrifice necessary beyond a single battle, It proves that the enemy is not only outside humanity but also the accumulated exhaustion of hearts that want everything to stop ]
entries.name: Erebus

entries.content: [ Elizabeth: a Velvet Room attendant who serves the elevator-shaped room with elegance, curiosity, and immense hidden power, Elizabeth observes the protagonist with formal politeness that gradually becomes personal fascination, Her later search beyond the Velvet Room centers on understanding the seal, the soul, and the human love that made sacrifice possible ]
entries.name: Elizabeth

entries.content: [ Kotone Shiomi: a second-year transfer student at Gekkoukan High School and Wild Card leader of SEES, Kotone returns to Tatsumi Port Island after losing her parents in the Moonlight Bridge incident and faces the Dark Hour with bright social energy over deep fatalism, She uses a naginata, awakens Orpheus, forms unusually direct bonds with teammates, and turns warmth into leadership without denying the death-haunted shape of her year ]
entries.name: Kotone Shiomi

entries.content: [ Justine: one of the twin wardens of Joker's prison-shaped Velvet Room, a small girl in a dark blue warden uniform and cap, platinum hair in a single long braid, golden eyes, an eyepatch, and a clipboard for recording the Inmate's progress, Cool, precise, and formally polite, she oversees fusion trials with detached curiosity, She is one half of Lavenza, split by Yaldabaoth's interference, and her composure hides a broken guidance system struggling to serve its guest ]
entries.name: Justine
